---
title: "1. Exploration"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
library(reticulate)
use_condaenv("stats")
```

## Histogram

A histogram is a simple way to visualise the distribution of a single continuous variable.

```{r}
# r code
abalone <- read.csv("abalone.csv")
hist(abalone$Weight, xlab="mass/g", main="Abalone mass", breaks=20)
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

abalone = pd.read_csv("abalone.csv")
fig, axs = plt.subplots(1, 1)
axs.hist(abalone["Weight"], bins=15)
axs.set_xlabel("mass/g")
axs.set_ylabel("Frequency")
axs.set_title("Abalone mass")
plt.show()

```
